product_name: Pip and Posy: The New Friend
rank_rationale: #1 over #2: third-friend jealousy is the sharpest 34–36m social wobble and Posy’s self-repair beats Little Whale’s sharing lesson on specificity, even though both clear the friendship brief.
include_rationale: Not another feelings chart — this is the moment your child watches their best friend laugh with someone else. Posy fixes it herself, which gives you language for real playgroup wobbles without you stepping in as referee.

age_raw: Preschool picture book; publisher compares to ages 3-5 sharing titles
rank_rationale: #2 over #3: Little Whale’s snack-and-attention sharing row is fresher and more playdate-specific than the chair-territory fight in That’s (Not) Mine, which many families already know.
include_rationale: Fresh 2024 pick for the tug-of-war over the good toy or the front of the queue. Less abstract than colour jars — a concrete story about turn-taking when friends come over.

rank_rationale: #3 over #4: the mine-shouting chair fight gives parents a same-day script after toy grabs, edging ahead of Sour Grape’s forgiveness arc which lands better once the row has cooled.
include_rationale: The 'that's MINE' phase meets friend play. Short, shouty text mirrors real arguments, then pivots to shared play — handy after playdates where nobody wanted to swap.

rank_rationale: #4 over #5: forgiveness after a friend fallout is a harder nearly-three skill than competitive sharing, so Sour Grape ranks above Squirrels even though both model repair.
include_rationale: When friend play goes wrong, nearly-threes can stay cross long after the toy is back. This is funny enough for repeat reads and gives you a light way into sorry and moving on.

rank_rationale: #5: competitive nut-chase teamwork is a strong backup social-repair story but less playdate-specific than the jealousy, sharing and mine-fight picks above.
include_rationale: When both children want the same thing and neither will budge, this rhyming chase gives you a way to talk about teamwork without sounding like a nursery poster.

product_name: Pip and Posy: The New Friend
rank_rationale: #1 over #2: third-friend jealousy is the sharpest 34–36m social wobble and Posy’s self-repair beats Little Whale’s sharing lesson on specificity, even though both clear the friendship brief.
